svg.title: Strategies in an Infinitely Repeated Prisoner's Dilemma

g.text: The Prisoner's Dilemma is a classic game theory paradox that has been extensively studied in the field of algorithmic game theory.
In this study material, we will focus on the strategies employed by players in an infinitely repeated Prisoner's Dilemma.
The Tit-for-Tat strategy is a simple and intuitive strategy that works by mimicking the other player's previous action.
The Alternative Strategies section explores the different strategies employed by players in an infinitely repeated Prisoner's Dilemma.
These strategies include the Suckers' Strategy, the Prail strategy, and the Grim Trigger strategy.
Each of these strategies has its own strengths and weaknesses, and players must carefully consider their options when making a decision.
In conclusion, the strategies employed by players in an infinitely repeated Prisoner's Dilemma are complex and multifaceted.
Players must carefully consider their options and choose a strategy that takes into account the long-term consequences of their actions.
By understanding the different strategies employed by players, we can gain insight into the underlying mechanisms of the Prisoner's Dilemma.

footer.text: This diagram was created using SVG and is intended for educational purposes only.
